content: When the LORD invited Solomon to ask anything, what did he ask for?
explanation: 1 Kings 3:9 — Solomon asked for an understanding heart to discern between good and evil. The LORD was pleased and added riches and honor.

content: On Mount Carmel, when Elijah challenged the prophets of Baal, what happened?
explanation: 1 Kings 18:38 — Fire from the LORD fell from heaven and consumed the offering, the wood, the stones, the dust, and the water. The people fell on their faces saying: 'The LORD, He is God!'
options: Fire fell from heaven and consumed the offering

content: How long did it take Solomon to build the temple?
explanation: 1 Kings 6:38 — The temple was built in seven years. A magnificent work.

content: At the dedication of the temple, what did Solomon declare about God?
explanation: 1 Kings 8:27 — Solomon understood that God is not confined to the temple. He is greater than any building.
options: God dwells only in the temple
Heaven and the highest heaven cannot contain You — much less this house

content: From which land did the queen come to test Solomon's wisdom?
explanation: 1 Kings 10:1 — The queen of Sheba came with hard questions. She was amazed at Solomon's wisdom and wealth.

content: Why did Solomon fall in his later years?
explanation: 1 Kings 11:4 — Solomon had 700 wives and 300 concubines, and they turned his heart toward foreign gods.
options: His foreign wives turned his heart away to other gods

content: Whose fault was it that the kingdom split?
explanation: 1 Kings 12:16 — Rehoboam listened to the young men's counsel and increased the burden. Ten tribes seceded; only Judah and Benjamin remained.
options: Rehoboam, for refusing to lighten the people's burden

content: What did the LORD use to feed Elijah by the brook Cherith?
explanation: 1 Kings 17:6 — Ravens brought Elijah bread and meat morning and evening. God provides in marvelous ways.

content: What miracle happened to the widow of Zarephath?
explanation: 1 Kings 17:16 — The jar of flour did not run out, the jug of oil did not fail. God provides when we obey.
options: Jar of flour and jug of oil did not run out throughout the famine
Her son rose from the dead

content: After his victory on Mount Carmel, how did Elijah react when Jezebel threatened him?
explanation: 1 Kings 19:4 — Even the greatest prophet has weak moments. The LORD tenderly cared for Elijah.
options: Fled in fear into the wilderness, asking to die

content: How did the LORD appear to Elijah at Mount Horeb?
explanation: 1 Kings 19:12 — The LORD was not in the wind, the earthquake, or the fire — but in a low whisper. God speaks in stillness.

content: How many had not bowed the knee to Baal, the LORD told Elijah?
explanation: 1 Kings 19:18 — Seven thousand had not bowed to Baal. Elijah was not as alone as he had thought.

content: How did Elijah call Elisha to be his disciple?
explanation: 1 Kings 19:19 — The prophet's cloak was a symbol of the transfer of office and power.

content: Who anointed Solomon king over Israel?
explanation: 1 Kings 1:39 — 'There Zadok the priest took the horn of oil from the tent and anointed Solomon. Then they blew the trumpet, and all the people said, Long live King Solomon!'

content: Whose wisdom did 1 Kings 4:29-30 say Solomon's wisdom surpassed?
explanation: 1 Kings 4:30 — 'Solomon's wisdom surpassed the wisdom of all the people of the east and all the wisdom of Egypt.'
options: All people of the east and Egypt

content: After Solomon finished the temple, what did the LORD promise on condition that he walked with Him?
explanation: 1 Kings 9:5 — 'I will establish your royal throne over Israel forever, as I promised David your father.'

content: The prophet Ahijah tore his garment into 12 pieces and gave Jeroboam how many?
explanation: 1 Kings 11:31 — 'Then he said to Jeroboam, Take for yourself ten pieces, for thus says the LORD, Behold, I am about to tear the kingdom from the hand of Solomon and will give you ten tribes.'

content: What did the man of God from Judah prophesy about the altar at Bethel?
explanation: 1 Kings 13:2 — 'O altar, altar, thus says the LORD: Behold, a son shall be born to the house of David, Josiah by name, and he shall sacrifice on you the priests of the high places.'
options: The altar would become a place of true worship
A descendant of David named Josiah will sacrifice the priests on it

content: Where did Rehoboam, son of Solomon, reign as king?
explanation: 1 Kings 14:21 — 'Now Rehoboam the son of Solomon reigned in Judah.' He had his capital at Jerusalem.

content: Whom did King Ahab have killed to seize his vineyard?
explanation: 1 Kings 21:13-14 — 'And they stoned him to death with stones.' Jezebel arranged a plot so Ahab could take Naboth's vineyard.
